alerts.title: Iran nuclear breakout window narrowed to 1-2 weeks; 60% U-235 enrichment confirmed
alerts.summary: Global metrics confirm Iran has achieved 60% uranium-235 enrichment with estimated breakout time of 1–2 weeks. This represents accelerated progress toward weapons-grade material amid collapsed IAEA inspection regime. Concurrent US-Iran nuclear talks in Switzerland remain stalled over missile program exclusion and inspection access to bombed sites (global_metrics, incidents).

alerts.title: US-Iran nuclear negotiations collapse over missile program and inspection disputes
alerts.summary: Trump administration publicly threatens military action against Iran even as delegations negotiate in Switzerland. Iran (via Foreign Ministry Spokesman Ismael Baqaei) categorically rejects IAEA inspector access to damaged nuclear facilities and refuses to include missile program in any agreement. Senate votes to block Trump from unilateral Iran strike authority. Multiple sources (Washington Post, NBC News, Fortune, RFE/RL, Ynetnews) report talks now paused due to escalatory threats (incidents).

alerts.title: Houthis escalate Red Sea operations; announce ban on Israeli vessel transit
alerts.summary: Houthis (Ansar Allah) attacked Israel and declared unilateral maritime blockade on Israeli vessels in the Red Sea. Coincides with elevated Houthi status in global proxy metrics (reported as 'Elevated – Multiple Recent Attacks'). Three US Navy destroyers (USS Carney, USS Mason, USS Laboon) remain deployed in Red Sea to counter such threats (incidents, assets).

alerts.title: Israeli airstrikes kill six in Gaza, three in Beirut suburbs amid ceasefire violations
alerts.summary: Israeli military strikes killed Al Jazeera cameraman and five others in Gaza City; concurrent Beirut suburbs strikes killed three including environmental activist Mona Khalil. Multiple independent sources (BBC, NBC News, New York Times, Al Jazeera, PBS) document breaches of Gaza-Lebanon ceasefire framework. Timing aligns with Hezbollah and Palestinian Islamic Jihad reported as 'Active' status (incidents).

alerts.title: US negative sentiment on Iran tensions rises; Trump-Iran narrative dominance increases
alerts.summary: US sentiment analysis shows 46% negative sentiment (up from baseline), with 'trump' (66 mentions), 'iran' (35), 'war' (16), and 'nuclear' (11) as top trending terms. Word frequency spike on nuclear and war terminology signals sustained public discourse tension around escalation risk. Concurrent Senate action to restrict Trump's Iran strike authority reflects domestic political polarization (sentiment).

analysis_note: Iran's nuclear program has reached critical threshold (60% enrichment, 1–2 week breakout window) while US-Iran negotiations have stalled over fundamental disagreements on inspections and missile limits; proxy escalation (Houthis, Israel-Gaza-Lebanon) and threatened US military action create imminent dual-track crisis risk.
